schools.summary: The LORD speaking concerning purification after childbirth: 'and the LORD spake unto Moses, saying' — the LORD speaking to Moses, now to give the law of purification after childbirth (vv.2-8, the speaking, God's command, the new law of the woman's purifying, the occasion, after the laws of clean and unclean foods, ch. 11, now the cleansing after birth, the continuation of the laws of purity, the LORD himself opening this law, showing that even the natural process of birth was brought under his ordering, the holiness of his people reaching into the most ordinary acts of life); the LORD speaking (the law of purification, the cleansing of the woman after childbirth) — the new law (the purifying after birth, the laws of cleanness continuing); the LORD spake unto Moses — the law of purification introduced (the providence, God ordering the cleansing after birth, the holiness of the people in all things) — Henry, Gill, Calvin, Ellicott.

themes: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying
the LORD speaking concerning purification after childbirth
God giving the law of purification after birth (vv.2-8)
the holiness of the people reaching into the ordinary acts of life

schools.summary: The uncleanness of childbirth: 'speak unto the children of Israel, saying, If a woman have conceived seed, and born a man child: then she shall be unclean seven days; according to the days of the separation for her infirmity shall she be unclean' — the meaning — 'she shall be unclean seven days': a woman who bore a child being ceremonially unclean (seven days for a son, two weeks for a daughter, vv.5), as in her monthly separation (the witnesses noting the significance — the uncleanness not because childbirth is sinful, it is the fulfilling of God's command, 'be fruitful and multiply,' and a blessing, Psalm 127:3, but a ceremonial figure, the lesson — the figure of inherited corruption, the uncleanness a token of the common corruption of our nature, that we are conceived and born in sin, the child brought forth a sinner, the taint of original sin passed down, 'behold, I was shapen in iniquity; and in sin did my mother conceive me,' Psalm 51:5, 'who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? not one,' Job 14:4, 'that which is born of the flesh is flesh,' John 3:6, the lesson — the need of cleansing from the very beginning, the inherited defilement requiring atonement, vv.6-8, the figure of original sin, that even the holy and blessed act of birth brings forth a child needing redemption); the uncleanness as in the monthly separation (the ceremonial impurity, the figure of the corruption of nature) — the figure (the inherited taint, the need of cleansing); the unclean seven days — the figure of inherited sin (the providence, the uncleanness a token of original corruption, the need of atonement, Psalm 51:5) — Calvin, Wesley, Keil, Gill.

debates.question: Why is the woman unclean after childbirth?

sides.position: Not because birth is sinful (it is a blessing and God's command), but a figure of the common corruption of our nature - that we are conceived and born in sin (Psalm 51:5; Job 14:4; John 3:6), the child brought forth a sinner needing cleansing from the beginning, a figure of original sin and the need of redemption

themes: If a woman have conceived seed, and born a man child: then she shall be unclean seven days
the uncleanness of childbirth (the crux of inherited corruption)
a figure of the common corruption of our nature, conceived and born in sin (Psalm 51:5; Job 14:4)
the child needing cleansing and redemption from the beginning (John 3:6)

schools.summary: Circumcision on the eighth day: 'and in the eighth day the flesh of his foreskin shall be circumcised' — circumcision — 'in the eighth day the flesh of his foreskin shall be circumcised': the male child to be circumcised on the eighth day (the witnesses noting the significance — circumcision the sign of the covenant, the token of God's covenant with Abraham and his seed, Genesis 17:10-14, 'this is my covenant... every man child among you shall be circumcised... and it shall be a token of the covenant,' the eighth day, the appointed day, after the seven days of the mother's first uncleanness, the child now received into the covenant, the Lord himself circumcised the eighth day, Luke 2:21, the lesson — the cutting away of the flesh, the figure of the putting off of the corruption of nature, the figure — the circumcision of the heart, the inward reality the outward sign pointed to, Deuteronomy 30:6, 'the LORD thy God will circumcise thine heart'; Romans 2:28-29, 'circumcision is that of the heart, in the spirit'; Colossians 2:11, 'in whom also ye are circumcised with the circumcision made without hands, in putting off the body of the sins of the flesh by the circumcision of Christ,' the putting off of the flesh, the dying to sin, the new birth, the seal of the covenant righteousness, Romans 4:11, circumcision 'a seal of the righteousness of the faith'); the circumcision (the covenant sign, the cutting away of the flesh, Genesis 17:10-14) — the figure (the putting off of the flesh, the circumcision of the heart, Colossians 2:11, the providence, the covenant sign, the inward reality fulfilled in Christ) — Ellicott, Clarke, Gill.

debates.question: What does circumcision on the eighth day signify?

sides.position: The sign and token of God's covenant with Abraham and his seed (Genesis 17:10-14), the child received into the covenant (Luke 2:21) - the cutting away of the flesh a figure of the circumcision of the heart and the putting off of the corruption of nature in Christ (Deuteronomy 30:6; Romans 2:28-29; Colossians 2:11), the seal of the righteousness of faith (Romans 4:11)

themes: And in the eighth day the flesh of his foreskin shall be circumcised
circumcision on the eighth day (the crux of the covenant sign)
the sign and token of God's covenant with Abraham and his seed (Genesis 17:10-14; Luke 2:21)
a figure of the circumcision of the heart and the putting off of the flesh in Christ (Colossians 2:11; Romans 4:11)

schools.summary: The days of purifying and the woman kept from the sanctuary: 'and she shall then continue in the blood of her purifying three and thirty days; she shall touch no hallowed thing, nor come into the sanctuary, until the days of her purifying be fulfilled' — after the seven days of full uncleanness (vv.2), the woman continuing thirty-three more days in her purifying (forty days in all for a son), during which she touched no holy thing nor came to the sanctuary (the thirty-three days, the longer period of purifying, the recovery and cleansing, the touch no hallowed thing, the woman kept from the holy things and the sanctuary until cleansed, the figure, the period of cleansing required, the holiness guarded); the woman kept from the sanctuary and the holy things (until the purifying was fulfilled) — the holiness (the holy things not approached in uncleanness, the reverence required, vv.7, the figure, the cleansing required before approaching God); 'until the days of her purifying be fulfilled' (the days completed, the cleansing finished, then the offering, vv.6, the figure, the period of cleansing, the reverence for the holy, the providence, the purifying fulfilled, the offering to follow) — Calvin, Wesley, Keil, Gill.

themes: And she shall then continue in the blood of her purifying three and thirty days; she shall touch no hallowed thing, nor come into the sanctuary, until the days of her purifying be fulfilled
the days of purifying and the woman kept from the sanctuary
the longer period of cleansing (forty days for a son), kept from the holy things
the cleansing required before approaching God, the reverence due to the holy

schools.summary: The doubled days of purifying for a daughter: 'but if she bear a maid child, then she shall be unclean two weeks, as in her separation: and she shall continue in the blood of her purifying threescore and six days' — for a daughter, the uncleanness and purifying doubled (two weeks of full uncleanness and sixty-six more days, eighty days in all, the doubled days, twice the period for a son, the as in her separation, the uncleanness as the monthly, the figure, the longer period for a daughter, variously explained — the witnesses noting it is not because the female is more sinful, but perhaps a ceremonial distinction, the corruption of nature inherited by all alike, the cleansing required for both); the doubled uncleanness for a daughter (twice the days, the ceremonial distinction) — the figure (the inherited corruption, the cleansing for both); 'she shall continue in the blood of her purifying threescore and six days' (the eighty days in all, the longer purifying for a daughter, the figure, the cleansing required, the corruption inherited by all, the providence, the period of purifying, the offering to follow, vv.6) — Ellicott, Gill, Wesley.

themes: But if she bear a maid child, then she shall be unclean two weeks, as in her separation: and she shall continue in the blood of her purifying threescore and six days
the doubled days of purifying for a daughter
the longer period for a daughter, a ceremonial distinction (not because the female is more sinful)
the corruption inherited by all and the cleansing required for both

schools.summary: The offering of purification: 'and when the days of her purifying are fulfilled, for a son, or for a daughter, she shall bring a lamb of the first year for a burnt offering, and a young pigeon, or a turtledove, for a sin offering, unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, unto the priest' — the offerings — 'a lamb... for a burnt offering, and a young pigeon, or a turtledove, for a sin offering': when the days were fulfilled, the woman bringing a lamb for a burnt offering and a bird for a sin offering (the witnesses noting the significance — the offering of atonement after childbirth, the sin offering for the cleansing, the burnt offering for the devotion, the lesson — the need of atonement even after the blessed act of birth, the uncleanness, the figure of inherited corruption, requiring a sin offering, the conscience cleansed, 'by one man sin entered into the world,' Romans 5:12, the need of atonement from the very entrance into the world, the burnt offering, the devotion and thanksgiving for the child, the figure — the sin offering and burnt offering, the atonement and the devotion, fulfilled in Christ, and the remarkable witness — the Lord's own mother brought this offering after his birth, Luke 2:22-24, 'when the days of her purification according to the law of Moses were accomplished, they brought him to Jerusalem... and to offer a sacrifice,' Mary submitting to the law, and the sinless Christ brought under the law born of a woman, Galatians 4:4); the burnt offering (the devotion) and the sin offering (the atonement) — the figure (the devotion and the atonement, fulfilled in Christ, the need, the cleansing required) — Wesley, Keil, Gill.

debates.question: Why is an offering of atonement required after childbirth?

sides.position: The need of atonement even after the blessed act of birth - the figure of inherited corruption requiring a sin offering (Romans 5:12), the conscience cleansed, fulfilled in Christ; the Lord's own mother brought this very offering after his birth (Luke 2:22-24), Mary submitting to the law, the sinless Christ born of a woman, made under the law (Galatians 4:4)

themes: She shall bring a lamb of the first year for a burnt offering, and a young pigeon, or a turtledove, for a sin offering, unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, unto the priest
the offering of purification (the crux of the need of atonement)
the need of atonement even after the blessed act of birth, the figure of inherited corruption (Romans 5:12)
the Lord's own mother brought this offering, the sinless Christ born under the law (Luke 2:22-24; Galatians 4:4)

schools.summary: The atonement made and the woman cleansed: 'who shall offer it before the LORD, and make an atonement for her; and she shall be cleansed from the issue of her blood. This is the law for her that hath born a male or a female' — the priest offering the sacrifice, making atonement, and the woman being cleansed (the atonement, the covering by the sacrifice, the cleansing, the cleansed, the woman purified, restored to the holy things and the sanctuary, the the law, the same law for a son or a daughter, the cleansing for both, the figure, the atonement and cleansing, fulfilled in Christ, Hebrews 9:14); the atonement and cleansing (the woman purified by the sacrifice, restored) — the figure (the cleansing through the atonement, the conscience purged, Hebrews 9:14, 'the blood of Christ... purge your conscience'); 'This is the law for her that hath born a male or a female' (the same law for both, the cleansing for son or daughter, the figure, the corruption inherited by all, the cleansing for all, the providence, the atonement and cleansing, the law for all) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill, Calvin, Ellicott.

themes: Who shall offer it before the LORD, and make an atonement for her; and she shall be cleansed from the issue of her blood. This is the law for her that hath born a male or a female
the atonement made and the woman cleansed
the atonement by the sacrifice and the woman purified, the same law for son or daughter
the figure of the cleansing of the conscience through the blood of Christ (Hebrews 9:14)

schools.summary: The provision for the poor: 'and if she be not able to bring a lamb, then she shall bring two turtles, or two young pigeons; the one for the burnt offering, and the other for a sin offering: and the priest shall make an atonement for her, and she shall be clean' — the mercy — 'if she be not able to bring a lamb, then she shall bring two turtles, or two young pigeons': if the woman was too poor to bring a lamb, she might bring two birds (one for the burnt offering, one for the sin offering, the witnesses noting the significance — the gracious provision for the poor, the way of cleansing adapted to the offerer's ability, the poor not excluded for want of means, the same atonement and cleansing for the poor, the lesson — God's mercy to the poor, the atonement made accessible to all, the lamb or, if too poor, the birds, the figure — the remarkable witness that the Lord's own mother brought this poor's offering after his birth, Luke 2:24, 'a pair of turtledoves, or two young pigeons,' Mary being poor, and the Lord of glory born into a poor family that brought the offering of the poor, the King of kings entering the world in poverty, 2 Corinthians 8:9, 'though he was rich, yet for your sakes he became poor,' the accessibility of cleansing to all, and the deep poverty of Christ's earthly estate); the two birds (one for the burnt offering, one for the sin offering, the devotion and the atonement) — the figure (the atonement for the poor, the same cleansing); 'the priest shall make an atonement for her, and she shall be clean' (the atonement and cleansing for the poor, the same as the rich, the equal pardon, the figure, the cleansing through the atonement, accessible to all, the providence, the poor cleansed, the mercy of God) — Wesley, Ellicott, Gill.

debates.question: What does the provision of birds for the poor teach?

sides.position: The gracious provision for the poor - the way of cleansing adapted to ability, none excluded by poverty, the same atonement for the poor; and the remarkable witness that the Lord's own mother brought this very poor's offering after his birth (Luke 2:24), Mary being poor, the Lord of glory born into poverty (2 Corinthians 8:9)

themes: And if she be not able to bring a lamb, then she shall bring two turtles, or two young pigeons; the one for the burnt offering, and the other for a sin offering: and the priest shall make an atonement for her, and she shall be clean
the provision for the poor (the crux of God's mercy and the Lord's own poor mother)
the way of cleansing adapted to ability, none excluded by poverty
the very offering the Lord's poor mother brought, the Lord of glory born into poverty (Luke 2:24; 2 Corinthians 8:9)
